summary: Iteration 7 verification: case-studies hero-depth alignment fix validated. All three brief pages (/approach, /discovery-process, /case-studies) now report identical eyebrow (.brief-page-hero .station-index) top coordinates at viewport 1440x900, confirming the new <main paddingTop: var(--header-h)> wrapper successfully aligns CaseStudies with the canonical pattern.

measurements.note: Main agent reported 310.39px; my run measures 296px consistently across all three. Absolute number differs slightly (likely due to header-h CSS var resolving differently in headless render), but cross-page equality (the actual assertion) is exact.

interaction_checks.search_filter_works: Typing 'manufacturing' filtered 68 -> 44 cards; clearing restored to 68
interaction_checks.industry_filter_works: Selecting 'Aerospace & Defense' filtered to 1 card; reset works
interaction_checks.card_click_no_errors: First card link clicked; 0 JS errors. (Note: first card has external href to thepowerscompany.com; internalRoute card not specifically isolated but no console errors during click cycle.)

critical_code_review_comments: Fix in CaseStudies.jsx (lines 97-225) is clean and minimal: wraps hero + controls + cards-grid in a single <main paddingTop: var(--header-h)> and renames the previous inner <main className=main-content> to <div>, avoiding double-main. Pattern now matches Approach.jsx line 34 and DiscoveryProcess.jsx exactly. No regressions observed in filter UI, cards rendering, or search behavior.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 7 verified case-studies hero-depth fix. All three canonical brief pages have identical .station-index top coords (296px on this rig, 0.00 delta). Single <main> on case-studies confirmed, cards-grid still renders 68 cards, search + industry filters functional, 0 JS errors. No further action required.
